item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty underwhelming. The core effect is good, even if it's not the greatest or most original. The static on cameras and teleporting part is pointless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a lot of coherence and writing issues. I'll be happy to edit if I get authorization from an admin. (I think the author is absent, if not I address directly to you).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like the picture got nuked for CC. But yeah, great idea, can only make this creepier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can see three ways to improve this. I think the author's gone, so it's not like I can change anything, but I feel like saying this.

  1. Get rid of the fucking "Number (#)" thing. That's annoying as hell.
  2. Get a picture. This could use a good picture.
  3. Change that line with the doctor getting his clearance to work on the skip removed. It can be smoother, like TL333s said above.

That being said, this is creepy as hell. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well. That's fuckin' creepy. I kind of feel like the last two tests should be switched for proper narrative buildup, and also the "Dr. blackbox can't test its awareness anymore" should be made less personal ("Further testing of SCP-1039's awareness is prohibited") to avoid the "Things Scruffy can't do in the army" feel. (Or whatever the heck that's called.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else notice this?

agent D-1039-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why I love this, but I really, really do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sinister

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My bad. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i believe that camera was sent back to the manufacturer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i believe windu was refering to this:

five (5) centimeter by five (5) centimeter cubes

they're either 5cm cubes - cubes necessarily being the same on all sides - or 5cm x 5cm x 5cm cubes (for the utmost clarity). listing two sides is just… ugh. don't do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems similar to another SCP whose number I can't recall, it was a camera that would teleport the subjects of its photos into the original packaging. Usually, reminding me of a previous entry makes me down-vote. But I like this doll a lot better than the camera, particularly the way it practically oozes with "screw you" attitude towards testing.

One detail, why does it get such a large room to itself? Could'nt they just lock it in a small box, maybe with a window so the cameras can keep an eye on it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not clear on which instance of "area" you're referring to. Do you mean this sentence?

"However, as SCP-1039 continued to transfer items into itself, the torso area of the object began to physically expand both vertically and horizontally to accommodate more items."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're welcome to rephrase and reformat any area of the article as you see fit. Just please refrain from adding to or detracting from the actual content of the story without consulting me first.

EDIT: I've done a minor edit splitting many of the massive WALL OF TEXT paragraphs into smaller, separate paragraphs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In experiment 1, should be volume, not area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this SCP. A great read. A shiver ran down my spine when I read the 'brain' part. Freaked me out. Brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The overlong paragraphs, tone problems, and usage issues are enough to earn my downvote. This could be a pretty okay SCP after a good round of revision & editing. -1 for now, but if you'd like my help with cleanup, you have only to ask.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Long paragraphs are loooooong.
Neutral vote for now.

item.title: Re: ...Awkward...
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps this could be a suitable replacement?

http://i.imgur.com/9BbC1.jpg

item.title: Re: ...Awkward...
item.content:encoded: It's still similar enough to prove a problem.

item.title: Re: ...Awkward...
item.content:encoded:
Well, shit. I'll just remove the image until I find a better one.

Thanks for the heads up.

EDIT: Added the new image. I hope this one hasn't been used before. Luckily, this new image doesn't conflict with the description.

item.title: ...Awkward...
item.content:encoded:
Hate to break it to you, but you're gonna need a new image:

http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/scp-670
